title: senecio obtectus
intro: Senecio obtectus, also known as woolly fireweed, is a flowering plant species native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as aiding in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Traditionally, Senecio obtectus was used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical applications, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
